danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring evasion or combat avoidance

danger_signals.visual_form: Spike traps, falling platforms, lava/water obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death or damage zones requiring precise platforming

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, more ornate enemy sprites with distinct animation patterns
Stage backgrounds transition to darker reds and blacks
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating danger or progression into enemy strongholds

safety_signals.visual_form: Platform chains without obstacles in immediate line of travel
safety_signals.meaning: Safe passage available; momentary reprieve from threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage transitions and enclosed safe rooms
Blue and yellow-dominant backgrounds (outdoor markets, palace courtyards)

faction_indicators.color_identity: Blue/red street clothes with white accents
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinctive tunicked silhouette with curved sword
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in marketplace and commoner-accessible areas

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red, gold, and purple armor palette
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ornate turbans, ornamental spears, palace insignia
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Guard sprites increase in armor complexity/size as stages progress; boss variants feature supernatural elements (Jafar's sorcery)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Concentrated in palace interior stages; red-tinted backgrounds signal faction control

faction_indicators.color_identity: Purple and dark blue (Jafar's magic influence)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Stylized magical effects, serpent motifs (Jafar's cobra form reference)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Abstract magical creatures; less grounded visual design than guards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appear in later stages; darker atmospheric transitions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright blue skies, sandy marketplace textures, minimal guard presence in backgrounds
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Scattered enemies; player-initiated encounters; open movement space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior architecture appears; gold and red color palette dominates; ornate backgrounds signal wealth/power
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased guard density; forced progression through threat zones; less freedom of movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Purple overlay effects; darker stage lighting; supernatural visual distortions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Magic-based enemies replace regular guards; stage mechanics become more hazardous; increased environmental threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dramatic lighting shift; black/dark purple backgrounds; large ornate boss arena
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single dominant enemy (Jafar); complex attack patterns; multiple phase transitions

environmental_storytelling.example: Movement from street-level marketplace (humble, accessible) through palace courtyards to interior chambers (ornate, restricted) communicates power hierarchy and Aladdin's transgressive journey
Stage backgrounds shift from warm yellows/blues (safety) to deep reds/purples (Jafar's domain), visually tracking player proximity to antagonist power
Guard sprites transition from human soldiers to magical constructs, showing Jafar's increasing supernatural influence over the world
Spike traps and environmental obstacles cluster near palace interiors, suggesting mechanical/magical defenses protecting stolen power

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Predictable left-right walking patterns with simple sprite flipping
Turns toward player and charges with faster animation speed
Complex multi-frame attack animations; telegraphed strike patterns; visible health state through animation intensity
Erratic movement patterns; projectile spawning; visual effects (sparkles, auras) indicate active threat state
Full-height sprite; brisk animation frames during movement
Sprite may show visual injury; movement animation slows; knockback effects more pronounced

notes: The Aladdin NES port exists in at least two versions (Virgin and unlicensed Hummer Team). This extraction is generalized to the platformer paradigm and thematic elements common to Disney's Aladdin adaptations. Visual communication relies on classic NES-era techniques: limited palette shifts for emotional/danger states, sprite size/detail as threat indicator, and architectural progression as narrative metaphor. The game communicates political/faction hierarchy primarily through environment (marketplace = common space; palace = restricted/guarded) and color language (Jafar's influence = purple/dark corruption). Danger signals are highly explicit due to platformer genre conventions (spikes, enemies, hazards must be visually clear).
sources_used: tcrf (Aladdin NES disambiguation page) - confirmed two versions exist; limited detailed visual data available from source
